goal: Visually verify the default-off operator dashboard after the eight-point read-only and contract-only integration stack, without adding runtime, provider, write, merge, or deploy behavior.
scope_out: Do not change dashboard implementation code, mount new routes, call providers, execute runtime, write DB, write GitHub, create PRs, merge branches, deploy, or change package/workflow files.
claims: The operator dashboard renders all eight integration panels.
Desktop and mobile layouts have no panel overlaps.
Desktop and mobile layouts have no horizontal overflow.
The only observed console error is the non-blocking favicon.ico 404.
assumptions: Visual review is performed against the local builder server with BLUEPILOT_OPERATOR_READ_ONLY_ROUTE_ENABLED=true.
This task records evidence only; permanent UI changes require a separate implementation lock if issues are found.
dod: Local /cockpit/operator-read-only route is opened in a browser.
Desktop and mobile screenshots are captured.
DOM layout measurements confirm 8 panels, expected statuses, no overlaps, and no horizontal overflow.
Focused tests, typecheck, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
stop_conditions: A dashboard code change, route mutation, provider call, runtime execution, durable write, GitHub write, PR creation, merge, deploy, package change, or workflow change becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: All eight integration points are read-only or contract-only; the operator dashboard now needs visual evidence before stacked branch review.
